text.title: Agency Acquisition Prep Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Agency Acquisition Prep plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The acquisition-prep cycle MUST measure founder-dependency: the share of revenue, decisions, and client relationships that pass through the founder alone, with a target ≤30%.
rule.rationale: Acquirers price down agencies where the founder is the product; reducing dependency is the highest-leverage prep work.

rule.statement: Each cycle MUST pick one of three lanes (strategic acquirer / financial buyer / aqui-hire) and reject mixed-lane prep activities for the cycle.
rule.rationale: Strategic and financial buyers value entirely different metrics; trying to optimise for both produces a deck that signals neither well.

rule.statement: Any inbound LOI MUST be reviewed under the standard hygiene checklist (exclusivity window, earnout cap, IP carve-outs, non-compete radius) before any verbal commitment.
rule.rationale: Verbal commitments to a single LOI eliminate competitive tension and tank valuation; LOI hygiene is the founder's primary leverage.

rule.statement: The data room MUST be maintained on a rolling weekly basis with the latest P&L, payroll roster, top-10 client retainer status, and IP inventory — not assembled reactively when an LOI arrives.
rule.rationale: Reactive data rooms are 30% incomplete and cost 6+ weeks; rolling data rooms close in 2-3 weeks.

rule.statement: Any checklist item without a named owner this cycle MUST be deferred with a written reason and aging counter — never silently skipped.
rule.rationale: Unowned items create the illusion of work; deferring + aging surfaces structural gaps (missing capability, wrong person) instead of hiding them.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
